morta_nuts2
  • Lee-Carter Parametric Model
  • View page source

Lee-Carter Parametric Model

Lee-Carter — Object-oriented architecture

Class hierarchy

LeeCarter                                  ← general base class
├── LeeCarter.Parametric                   ← parametric Lee-Carter (B-splines + P-splines)
│   └── LeeCarter.Parametric.National      ← β_x shared across all regions
└── LeeCarter.Classic                      ← classic Lee-Carter (gradient descent)

USAGE

# National parametric model
model = LeeCarter.Parametric.National(n_knots=10, lam=0.1)
results = model.fit(ax_coef_init, bx_coef_init, kappa_init, Extg, Dxtg, xv, tv)

# Classic model
model = LeeCarter.Classic(nb_iter=500)
results = model.fit(ax, bx, kappa, Extg, Dxtg, xv, tv)

USES STANDARD PYTHON LIBRARIES

✓ scipy.interpolate.BSpline         — B-spline construction
✓ scipy.interpolate.make_lsq_spline — least squares fit
✓ scipy.special.gammaln             — log-factorial
✓ scipy.linalg                      — linear algebra
✓ numpy                             — matrix computation
class morta_nuts2.model.LC_p.lcp_class.LeeCarter[source]

Bases: object

General Lee-Carter class.

Groups together :

  • shared mathematical tools (static methods)

  • the two main model families as nested sub-classes :

    · LeeCarter.Parametric (B-splines, P-splines) · LeeCarter.Classic (non-parametric, gradient descent)

Quick usage example

>>> model = LeeCarter.Parametric.National(n_knots=10, lam=0.1, verbose=True)
>>> results = model.fit(ax0, bx0, kappa0, Extg, Dxtg, xv, tv)
static make_penalty_matrix(n_basis, diff_order=2)[source]

Build the P-spline penalty matrix \(D^T D\).

The penalty matrix is constructed from a finite difference operator applied to the spline coefficients. It is used to enforce smoothness in the spline representation.

The penalization term is:

\[P(c) = \lambda \, c^T (D^T D) c\]

where:

  • \(c\) are the spline coefficients

  • \(D\) is the finite difference matrix

  • \(\lambda\) is the smoothing parameter

The matrix \(D\) approximates discrete derivatives of the spline coefficients and is constructed using numpy.diff.

Parameters:

  • n_basis (int) – Number of spline basis functions.

  • diff_order (int) –

    Order of the finite difference operator.

    • 1 → first-order differences (penalizes slope changes)

    • 2 → second-order differences (penalizes curvature)

Returns:

  • DtD (numpy.ndarray of shape (n_basis, n_basis)) – Quadratic penalty matrix used in the penalized likelihood.

  • diag_DtD (numpy.ndarray of shape (n_basis,)) – Diagonal of \(D^T D\), often used for Hessian approximation or diagonal preconditioning.

Notes

This matrix is used in the penalized likelihood:

\[\ell_p(\theta) = \ell(\theta) - \lambda c^T D^T D c\]

which controls the smoothness of the spline coefficients.

static poisson_lnL(Dxtg, Extg, logmu, logDxtgFact)[source]

Poisson log-likelihood (equation 2 of the paper). Uses scipy.special.gammaln for log(D!).

static update_ax_coef(ax_coef, B, residual, weighted_exp, eta, lam, DtD, diag_DtD)[source]

NR update of B-spline coefficients of α_x.

static update_kappa(kappa, bx_reg, residual, weighted_exp, eta)[source]

NR update of κ_t.

static compute_fit_stats(Dxtg, Extg, logmu, logDxtgFact, n_basis, nb_years, nb_regions, lam=0.0, DtD=None, B=None, weighted_exp=None)[source]

Computes Poisson deviance, AIC, BIC.

When lam > 0 and B, DtD, weighted_exp are provided, the effective degrees of freedom (edf) are used instead of the nominal n_basis, so that AIC/BIC correctly penalize the P-spline smoothing.

Effective dofs per spline component:

edf = trace( B (B^T W B + 2λ D^T D)^{-1} B^T W )

where W = diag(weighted_exp summed over t and g).

dofs_mod = edf_ax + edf_bx + nb_years

↑ α_x ↑ β_x ↑ κ_t (unpenalized)

static build_input_from_dataframe(df)[source]

Transforms a long DataFrame into 3D matrices compatible with .fit().

Parameters:

df (DataFrame with columns) – [‘region’, ‘year’, ‘age’, ‘deaths’, ‘exposure’, ‘mortality_rate’]

Returns:

  • Muxtg ((nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions))

  • Dxtg ((nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions))

  • Extg ((nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions))

  • xv (sorted age vector)

  • tv (sorted year vector)

  • regions (list of regions)

class Parametric[source]

Bases: object

Parametric Lee–Carter model using B-splines — national variant.

The mortality intensity is modeled as:

\[\ln(\mu_{x,t,g}) = \alpha_x + \beta_x \cdot \kappa_t\]

where \(\beta_x\) is common to all regions (no g dimension).

Age effects are represented using B-spline basis functions in order to obtain smooth age profiles.

Notes

The multi-region variant (β_{x,g} per region), which corresponds to the Lee & Li model, has been moved to llp_class.py → LeeAndLi.

The spline representation allows flexible modeling of age patterns while controlling smoothness through a penalization term (P-splines).

This approach improves stability compared to the classical Lee–Carter model when mortality data are sparse or noisy.

class National(degree=2, n_knots=10, xmin=None, xmax=None, lam=0.0, diff_order=2, nb_iter=800, eta0=0.2, tol=0.001, verbose=False)[source]

Bases: object

Parametric Lee-Carter — national variant. β_x is common to all regions (no g dimension).

Parameters:

  • degree (int B-spline degree)

  • n_knots (int number of internal knots)

  • xmin/xmax (float age bounds (inferred from data if None))

  • lam (float P-splines penalty)

  • diff_order (int penalty difference order)

  • nb_iter (int max iterations)

  • eta0 (float initial learning rate)

  • tol (float convergence tolerance)

  • verbose (bool)

static compute_logmu_national(ax_coef, bx_coef, kappa, xv, B)[source]

ln(µ_{x,t,g}) = α_x + β_x · κ_t β_x common to all regions.

Parameters:

  • ax_coef ((n_basis,))

  • bx_coef ((n_basis,) ← no region dimension)

  • kappa ((nb_years,))

  • xv ((nb_ages,))

  • B ((nb_ages, n_basis))

Returns:

  • logmu ((nb_ages, nb_years, 1) broadcast over g)

  • ax ((nb_ages,))

  • bx ((nb_ages,))

static update_bx_coef_national(bx_coef, B, kappa, residual, weighted_exp, eta, lam, DtD, diag_DtD)[source]

NR update of β_x (common to all regions). residual / weighted_exp: (nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions) Sum over regions to aggregate the gradient.

static rescale_bx_kappa_national(bx_coef, bx, kappa)[source]

Normalization: Σ_x β_x = 1

fit(ax_coef_init, bx_coef_init, kappa_init, Extg, Dxtg, xv, tv)[source]
class Classic(nb_iter=500, eta=1)[source]

Bases: object

Classic (non-parametric) Lee-Carter model fitted by gradient descent.

ln(µ_{x,t}) = α_x + β_x · κ_t

Parameters:

  • nb_iter (int number of gradient descent iterations)

  • eta (float learning rate)

fit(ax, bx, kappa, Extg, Dxtg, xv, tv)[source]
